Dear Mr. Kelly: 
Thank you for your letter of September 1, 1976, inquiring 
about the development of Conflict of Interest Codes for city 
agencies and the appropriate Code reviewing body for the "Civic 
Center Authori ty" • 
A package of materials outlining various model Codes and 
instructions for developing Conflict of Interest Codes will be 
led to you shortly, including materials explaining recent 
Commission actions regarding development of Conflict of Interest 
Codes. 
The Commission has not undertaken any projects toward 
developing specific Codes for various City departments; 
however, the methods of categorizing various designated 
employees will be set forth in each of the model Codes 
transmitted in the package of materials your City will 
soon be receiving. 
You ask, in addition, who is the Code reviewing body for 
the Civic Center Authority, which is created and 
tered

Based upon this portion of the Government Code, it is 
clear that the Conflict of Interest Code of the San Bernar-
dino Civic Center Authority will be reviewed by the Board 
of Supervisors. 
If I may be of further assistance to you in any regard, 
please feel free to call me at 916/322-6444.

sections.question: It would be appreciated if you would forward proposed Conflict of Interest Codes for various city departments. We are especially interested in methods of categorizing the various "designated employees". Additionally, City of San Bernardino, the Redevelopment Agency of the City of San Bernardino and the County of San Bernardino have entered into a joint powers agreement creating the San Bernardino Civic Center Authority. That body has acquired, constructed and leased public buildings. Who is the Code Reviewing Body for the Civic Center Authority?
sections.conclusion: Based upon this portion of the Government Code, it is clear that the Conflict of Interest Code of the San Bernardino Civic Center Authority will be reviewed by the Board of Supervisors.
sections.question_synthetic: The Deputy City Attorney of San Bernardino requests FPPC model Conflict of Interest Codes with guidance on categorizing designated employees, and asks which body should review the Conflict of Interest Code for a Civic Center Authority created by joint powers agreement between the city, redevelopment agency, and county.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that the Board of Supervisors is the appropriate Code reviewing body for the Civic Center Authority, as established by Government Code §82011. The FPPC also committed to sending model Conflict of Interest Codes and materials explaining methods of categorizing designated employees.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (dated September 1, 1976) and FPPC response (dated September 28, 1976). QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections were extracted verbatim from the incoming request and response respectively. Document uses government code citation and formal advice format typical of FPPC letters.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: It would be appreciated if you would forward proposed Conflict of Interest Codes for various city departments. We are especially interested in methods of categorizing the various "designated employees". Additionally, City of San Bernardino, the Redevelopment Agency of the City of San Bernardino and the County of San Bernardino have entered into a joint powers agreement creating the San Bernardino Civic Center Authority. That body has acquired, constructed and leased public buildings. Who is the Code Reviewing Body for the Civic Center Authority?

CONCLUSION: Based upon this portion of the Government Code, it is clear that the Conflict of Interest Code of the San Bernardino Civic Center Authority will be reviewed by the Board of Supervisors.

embedding.summary: FPPC Chief Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr. responded to San Bernardino's Deputy City Attorney regarding Conflict of Interest Code development and identified the Board of Supervisors as the appropriate reviewing body for the Civic Center Authority created by joint powers agreement. The FPPC also committed to providing model Codes and categorization guidance materials.
